description: Update all documentation files to reflect the actual current state of the repo: (1) Mark Feast Feature Store as partially implemented (feature_repo/ exists with parquet files, feature definitions, feature_store.yaml, and tests). (2) Add deployment time and cost estimates section to README.md and README.es.md. (3) Correct backlog.md item #34 which falsely claims parquet files are missing. (4) Update VALIDATION_REPORT.md checklist to mark Feature Store as partially done. (5) Ensure docs are clear that AWS account config should NOT be in the repo, only documented how to configure externally.
steps: In `docs/PENDING.md`: Move 'Feature Store con Feast' from Baja prioridad to a new partially-completed state. Add a note under Completado section: '[x] Feature Store con Feast (parcial) - Feature repo local con Feast: definiciones, datos parquet, online_store.db, registry.db, tests. Falta: backend productivo (Redis/DynamoDB), server deployment en K8s (2026-06-XX)'. Update the Baja prioridad item to say 'Feature Store con Feast - Backend productivo (Redis/DynamoDB), server deployment, integracion con pipelines de training'.

In `backlog.md`: Fix item #34 in MEDIUM section which says 'Feast feature repo: faltan archivos parquet fuente' -- this is WRONG. The files DO exist at `ml-platform/feature_repo/data/` (model_features.parquet, online_store.db, registry.db, transaction_stats.parquet, user_profile.parquet). Remove this item or replace it with 'Feast feature repo: falta backend productivo (Redis/DynamoDB) y deployment en K8s'. Update the dashboard counts accordingly.

In `VALIDATION_REPORT.md`: Update the checklist item '[ ] Feature Store con Feast' to '[x] Feature Store con Feast (parcial) - Definiciones locales + datos parquet + tests (2026-06-XX). Falta backend productivo.'

In `README.md`: Add a new section '## Deployment Time & Cost Estimates' before the Contributing section with: EKS Control Plane $0.10/hr, NAT Gateway $0.045/hr, 2x m5.large nodes $0.192/hr each, Total for a 3-hour E2E test ~$2.50-$4.00 USD. Time estimates: Terraform apply 15-25 min, MLOps stack deploy 10-15 min, Full validation 10-15 min, Terraform destroy 10-15 min, Total ~1 hour.
In `README.es.md`: Add the equivalent Spanish section '## Estimados de Tiempo y Costo de Despliegue' with the same data in Spanish.

In `implementation.md` section 11 (Known Limitations & Pending Items): Move 'Feature Store with Feast' from Pending to Implemented (partial). Add note about local Feast repo with parquet data being functional.
In `gitops/IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md` Tasks Pendientes section: Update the Feast item to indicate local feature store is implemented, only production backend deployment is pending.
Ensure no AWS account IDs or credentials are documented anywhere in the READMEs - just instructions to run `aws configure` externally. This should already be correct; verify and leave as-is if so.
acceptance_criteria: docs/PENDING.md correctly shows Feast feature store as partially completed with date stamp
backlog.md no longer claims parquet files are missing (item #34 corrected)
VALIDATION_REPORT.md checklist marks Feature Store as partially done
README.md has a 'Deployment Time & Cost Estimates' section with dollar amounts and time breakdowns
README.es.md has equivalent Spanish section with same data
implementation.md lists Feature Store as partially implemented
gitops/IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md pending section updated for Feast
No AWS account IDs or credentials in any documentation file
verification: grep -l 'Cost Estimates\|Costo de Despliegue' README.md README.es.md
grep -c 'faltan archivos parquet' backlog.md should return 0
